channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: XBOX Expects Further Price Increases in 2027
item.content:encoded:
XBOX has just announced a price increase for its consoles, and expects the storage and memory component costs to double in Fall 2027.

The latest set of XBOX console price increases has been announced. Starting August 1, the price of 512GB models will increase by $100, and the price of 1TB models will increase by $150. The price increase comes as part of rising component costs, and those prices could rise again in 2027.

XBOX Expects Console Storage and Memory Prices to Double in 2027

In a recent blog post, Xbox announced that it will need to raise console prices due to rising costs of storage and memory components. The company noted that these prices have increased by more than 2.5 times and expects them to double by Fall 2027. Microsoft also highlighted that the surge in component costs has a particularly significant impact on consoles, as they are usually sold at a loss, meaning they cost more to produce than they sell for.

  • RELATED: GTA 6 XBOX Files Hint At New GTA Online Multiplayer

The increasing costs of memory and storage components are impacting all major players, forcing them to adjust their prices. Earlier this year, we saw Sony raise prices on all PlayStation hardware. We also saw the price announcement for the highly anticipated Steam Machine, which starts at $1,000. Valve explained that the Steam Machine was originally intended to be much cheaper; however, shortages and significant price increases in RAM and storage components led to higher costs.

What are your thoughts on XBOX expecting further price increases in 2027? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Keen Games revealing a release date for Enshrouded 1.0. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Enshrouded 1.0 Release Date Revealed
item.content:encoded:
A new announcement confirms that Enshrouded 1.0 will be released for PC, Playstation 5, and PlayStation 5 Pro on October 15, 2026. XBOX release is set for Spring 2027.

Over the last few years, we have been hearing about all the updates the game has had during its early access period. However, Keen Games GmbH has announced that it is finally time for full release, with the 1.0 update arriving this Autumn. The developers have also released a teaser with the announcement.

Enshrouded 1.0 Release Date Teases New Features

With the confirmation of Enshrouded 1.0’s release date, the developers also teased some of the upcoming features in the new teaser video. The teaser video featured multiple gameplay clips, which included glimpses of combat, open-world exploration, and some of the building mechanics. The last clip showcased the player overlooking the world of Enshrouded. Interested readers can check out Enshrouded 1.0’s teaser video below:

The developers have added that information on improvements, optimizations, QoL changes, and that all the new stuff will be arriving “later this summer.”

What is Enshrouded

Enshrouded is a co-op survival action RPG set in an open world that includes base-building mechanics. In the game, you play as the Flameborn, the last hope of a dying race. The vast open world presents various challenges and offers many opportunities for players to scavenge resources. The building mechanics enable players to construct grand structures, customize every detail, and create shelters for NPCs who unlock workshops and provide the tools needed to craft epic gear.

The game includes intense combat against various corrupted factions, as well as challenging boss fights. Players can engage in hand-to-hand combat, dodge attacks, parry, and utilize a range of powerful skills and spells to defeat enemies. There are various crafting mechanics that allow players to create and upgrade legendary weapons and gear. Additionally, you can raid, build, and battle in 16-player co-op mode.

What are your thoughts on Enshrouded 1.0’s release date? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the XBOX announcing another price increase for consoles. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Gray Zone Warfare Hits 1.5 Million Copies Sold and Gets a Major Update
item.content:encoded:
Gray Zone Warfare, the open-world tactical MMOFPS, has received a major update as the team at MADFINGER unveils a whopping 1.5 million copies sold since April 2024. The update, which comes in the form of patch 0.4.5.0, delivers a PvP-focused overhaul to the game, along with several community-requested tweaks.

Higher-stakes PvP has been promised following the arrival of 0.4.5.0, and to further celebrate this new update and milestone, MADFINGER is hosting a major sale on Steam and a Twitch Drops campaign.

Gray Zone Warfare Continues to Evolve

Gray Zone Warfare turned a corner when the 0.4 update arrived at the end of March. It was a monumental transformation for the game that brought about the biggest overhaul since the title dropped into early access in April 2024.

In my review, I showered the game in much-deserved praise:

Gray Zone Warfare represents a stunning example of what happens when a dedicated, devoted developer expresses nothing but passion for the core product they have created. It’s without a doubt a best-in-class title that has untold amounts of potential in store, and after the 0.4 update, it’s an open-world tactical shooter that simply must be experienced to be believed.

Now, Gray Zone Warfare has climbed to 1.5 million sales, and to celebrate that milestone, MADFINGER has released the 0.4.5.0 update.

This update mostly targets the game’s PvP-focused ecosystem, which is one of the driving reasons for venturing into the wild and exploring the vast world of Lamang.

First, Conflict Zones have been expanded, stretching to 400 meters from the previous 300 meters. The team explained this change in a press release:

This change prevents attacking players from having their approximate position revealed when a PvP encounter begins, allowing the attacking side to maintain their advantage.

Respawn logic has been reworked so that players engaging in PvP and winning their fights have more time to secure the area and loot their kills before those they eliminated make their way back into the Conflict Zone.

The recapture cooldown on Combat Outposts has been altered too, so that factions must wait 30 minutes before they can recapture it, which prevents rapid flip-flopping between faction occupation.

In terms of community-requested features, Task Sharing has now been added, encouraging folks to squad up and work together to complete their side missions.

Additionally, the 0.4.5.0 patch adds support for DLSS 4.5 and introduces fixes and improvements across all gameplay systems.

Have you given Gray Zone Warfare a try? Let us know what you think about the game on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the Resident Evil and Monster Hunter collab, and be sure to subscribe to the Insider Gaming Newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill
